danger_signals.visual_form: Biological/organic enemy sprites with irregular shapes; virus-like particle effects
danger_signals.meaning: Active infection and computational threat; dangerous corrupted entities

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards with biological appearance contaminating level layout
danger_signals.meaning: World-state corruption; zones of active viral spread requiring navigation or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Single enclosed room at level end with distinct visual presentation
danger_signals.meaning: Critical threat nodes; escalated danger requiring defeat to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: UI display of resource consumption; higher costs = more exotic weapons
danger_signals.meaning: Strategic danger assessment; rare weapons are resource-intensive, limiting availability

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items within levels displaying chip/power-up visual identity
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation enabling offensive capability and special weapons

safety_signals.visual_form: Sequential room-based level design with clear boundaries and checkpoints
safety_signals.meaning: Containment and predictability; players can understand threat zones clearly

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kabuki actor transformation/ancestral imagery (red hair as weapon identity marker)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Digital/computational aesthetic overlaid with kabuki cultural references

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Biological/organic visual language; mutant creature designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mutant creatures, parasite environments, viral debris patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Contaminated digital space with organic/biological manifestations; final boss revealed as alien-origin entity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Binary code conceptual framework; computational environment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Virtual world interior aesthetic; digital hazards masked as biological threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clean digital/computational environment (inferred from contrast with corrupted state)
Biological debris scattered throughout levels; organic-looking hazards and enemy types; mutant creature populations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostile enemy behavior; environmental hazards actively damage player; virus progressively corrupts remaining systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Escalating biological contamination across sequential levels; mutation intensity increases toward final boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy difficulty progression; final level reveals alien origin and offensive capability (Hyperion probe weapon threat)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Implied restoration of clean computational environment (game end state)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cessation of threat; prevention of Hyperion laser weapon activation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Biological contamination density as threat indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Viral debris and parasite environments increase in visual prevalence through level progression, communicating escalating infection severity

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type variation as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Mutant creatures represent different manifestations of viral corruption; variety suggests different infection vectors or adaptation stages
Virtual world interior (protagonist's brain transferred to binary code) creates juxtaposition where biological threats appear anomalous, emphasizing alien nature of virus
Player avatar manifests as kabuki actor ancestor when entering computer system; visual form communicates cultural/historical resonance within technological setting
Single enclosed room per level containing boss communicates quarantine-like threat concentration and spatial organization of viral nodes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hostile engagement with player; patrolling contaminated environments
Environmental hazards causing damage on contact; static or patterned obstacle behavior
Contained in single room; escalated attack patterns and health pools; defeat enables level progression
Static environmental clutter; visual noise indicating infection presence without direct threat
Hair-based melee attacks; chip-based special weapons; movement through infected digital space

notes: Kabuki: Quantum Fighter communicates world-state primarily through biological contamination visuals in a digital setting. The core narrative tension—alien virus infecting Earth's defense computer—manifests through enemy design (mutant creatures), environmental hazards (parasitic contamination), and level progression (escalating threat). The kabuki actor visual identity of the player avatar creates cultural-technological juxtaposition that emphasizes the alien nature of viral threats. Regional differences (Japanese version as Zipang film tie-in vs. international standalone release) suggest visual storytelling was adapted but core threat manifestation remained consistent. Chip-based weapon system creates resource scarcity mechanics that reinforce danger assessment gameplay.
